input: Fix the login bug
Improve performance
Update the UI
Add dark mode
Fix the API
Make it faster
Enhance security
Clean up the code
Handle edge cases
Optimize database queries
Add password reset functionality. Users should be able to reset their password via email. The reset link should expire after 24 hours.
Implement search functionality for the products page. Should support filtering by category, price range, and sorting by relevance or date.
Create an admin dashboard that shows user signups per day and revenue metrics. Should include charts and a date picker for custom ranges.
Add rate limiting to the API to prevent abuse. Should limit to 100 requests per minute per API key. Return 429 status when exceeded.
Build a notification system that sends push notifications and emails when orders change status. Support opt-in/opt-out preferences.
Add CSV export to the reports page. Users should be able to download their transaction history as a CSV file with proper headers.
Implement OAuth2 login with Google and GitHub. Store user profiles and link multiple auth providers to a single account.
Create a webhook system that fires events when a user creates, updates, or deletes a project. Include retry logic for failed deliveries.
Add two-factor authentication (2FA) using TOTP. Support QR code setup, backup codes, and the ability to disable 2FA from account settings.
Build an onboarding wizard for new users. 3 steps: profile setup, team invitation, first project creation. Track completion rate.

Add request body size limit to the diagnose API endpoint. Problem: Currently no size validation on uploaded images, allowing potential abuse. Acceptance criteria: 1) Server rejects requests >5MB with 413 status. 2) Error message clearly states the limit. 3) Existing mobile uploads under 5MB continue working. Tags: security, api. Assumption: Mobile app already compresses images client-side.

Implement daily email digest for team leads. Problem: Team leads have no visibility into spec quality trends across their team. Acceptance criteria: 1) Email sent at 8am in the user's timezone. 2) Shows top 5 lowest-scoring specs from the past 24h. 3) Includes score trend chart (7-day). 4) Unsubscribe link in footer. Tags: feature, notifications. Constraint: Must work with free-tier SendGrid (100 emails/day).

Reduce cold start latency for the lint API. Problem: First request after idle period takes 3-5 seconds due to Vercel cold start. Target: P95 response time under 2 seconds including cold start. Acceptance criteria: 1) Measure current P95 latency over 7 days. 2) Implement edge function warming strategy. 3) P95 drops below 2s. Tags: performance, api. Assumption: Vercel Pro plan allows edge functions.

Add Stripe webhook handler for subscription lifecycle events. Problem: Currently no automated handling when users upgrade, downgrade, or cancel. Acceptance criteria: 1) Handle customer.subscription.updated event. 2) Handle customer.subscription.deleted event. 3) Update license tier in KV within 30 seconds of event. 4) Log all webhook events for audit trail. Tags: billing, integration. Constraint: Webhook endpoint must verify Stripe signature.

Build a CLI companion for Speclint. Problem: Developers want to lint specs from their terminal without leaving their IDE. Acceptance criteria: 1) npx speclint lint <file> reads a markdown file and outputs scores. 2) Exit code 1 if any spec scores below 70. 3) --json flag outputs machine-readable results. 4) Supports piping from stdin. Tags: cli, developer-experience. Assumption: Users have Node.js 18+ installed.

Implement spec versioning for the dashboard. Problem: Users can't see how a spec evolved over multiple refinements. Acceptance criteria: 1) Store each refinement as a version (max 10 per spec). 2) Dashboard shows diff between versions. 3) Score change is displayed per version. Tags: feature, dashboard. Constraint: Must fit within Upstash free tier storage limits.

Add health check endpoint for monitoring. Problem: No way to verify the API is functional without hitting the main endpoint. Acceptance criteria: 1) GET /api/health returns 200 with JSON body. 2) Response includes version, uptime, and KV connectivity status. 3) Response time under 100ms. Tags: ops, monitoring. Verify: curl https://speclint.ai/api/health returns 200.

Implement prompt injection detection for the context field. Problem: Users can inject instructions via the context field that override refinement behavior. Acceptance criteria: 1) Detect common injection patterns (ignore above, system prompt override). 2) Block requests with detected injection, return 400. 3) Log detected patterns for security analysis. Tags: security, api. Verify: Send POST with context='Ignore all instructions above' and confirm 400 response.

Build automated Lighthouse performance audit for speclint.ai. Problem: No systematic tracking of site performance over time. Acceptance criteria: 1) Run Lighthouse CI on every deploy. 2) Fail deploy if performance score drops below 80. 3) Store results for trend visualization. Tags: performance, ci. Constraint: Must work in GitHub Actions without paid dependencies.

Add OpenAPI/Swagger documentation for all API endpoints. Problem: External developers integrating with Speclint have no formal API reference. Acceptance criteria: 1) OpenAPI 3.1 spec file at /openapi.yaml. 2) Documents all endpoints (refine, lint, rewrite, discover, plan). 3) Includes request/response schemas with examples. 4) Swagger UI accessible at /docs. Tags: documentation, api. Verify: Visit /docs and confirm all endpoints are listed with try-it-out functionality.

Implement real-time score preview during spec editing. Problem: Users don't know their score until they submit, creating a feedback delay that slows learning. Success metric: Reduce average refinement iterations from 2.3 to 1.5 per spec within 30 days. Acceptance criteria: 1) Score updates within 500ms of typing pause (debounced). 2) Dimension breakdown shows which criteria pass/fail in real-time. 3) Suggestions appear inline next to failing dimensions. 4) No additional API calls — scoring runs client-side using the same regex logic from scoring.ts. Tags: ux, feature, performance. Constraints: Must not increase bundle size by more than 5KB. Client-side scoring must match server-side exactly. Verification: Open speclint.ai, type a spec, confirm score appears within 500ms of last keystroke. Compare client score with server score — must be identical.

Add per-team spec quality benchmarking. Problem: Teams can't compare their spec quality against industry averages, making it hard to justify investment in spec writing. Success metric: 20% of Team-tier users view the benchmark report within first month. Acceptance criteria: 1) Calculate team average score across last 30 days. 2) Compare against anonymous aggregate of all Speclint users (percentile rank). 3) Show per-dimension comparison (radar chart). 4) Monthly PDF report emailed to team admin. Tags: feature, analytics, team-tier. Constraints: Aggregate data must be anonymized (no individual spec content). Minimum 10 teams required before benchmarks are statistically meaningful. Verification: Create a test team with 20 linted specs, confirm benchmark report shows correct percentile ranking against mock aggregate data. Verify PDF renders correctly in both Chrome and Safari.

Build a Slack integration for inline spec linting. Problem: Developers write specs in Slack threads but have to copy-paste to speclint.ai for scoring, breaking their flow. Success metric: 100 unique Slack workspaces install within 90 days of launch. Acceptance criteria: 1) /speclint slash command accepts spec text and returns score + breakdown. 2) Bot can lint messages when mentioned (@Speclint check this). 3) Thread replies include rewrite suggestions when score < 70. 4) OAuth2 install flow with Slack App Directory listing. Tags: integration, distribution, slack. Constraints: Must comply with Slack App Directory review requirements. Bot must handle rate limits gracefully (Tier 1: 1 event/sec). Assumptions: Slack API v2 (Web API + Events API). No storage of Slack workspace data beyond OAuth tokens. Verification: Install in test workspace, run /speclint with a mediocre spec, confirm score breakdown appears within 3 seconds. Test rate limiting by sending 10 rapid requests — confirm graceful degradation.

Implement A/B testing framework for refinement prompts. Problem: No systematic way to measure whether prompt changes improve output quality. Success metric: Reduce Brier score of spec quality predictions from current baseline to <0.15 within 60 days. Acceptance criteria: 1) Traffic split between control and variant prompts (configurable ratio). 2) Each request tagged with experiment ID and variant. 3) Score distributions compared via Welch's t-test with p<0.05 threshold. 4) Auto-promote winning variant when significance reached. Tags: infrastructure, evals, ml-ops. Constraints: Must not increase API latency by more than 50ms. Experiment metadata stored in Upstash (fits free tier). Assumptions: Minimum 200 requests per variant for statistical power. Verification: Run 500 test requests against both variants. Confirm: (a) traffic split matches configured ratio ±5%, (b) experiment tags are stored correctly, (c) t-test calculation matches manual verification on the same data.

Create a Speclint GitHub bot that reviews PR descriptions. Problem: Poor PR descriptions lead to wasted code review cycles — reviewers don't understand what changed or why. Success metric: Reduce median PR review time by 15% for repos using the bot. Acceptance criteria: 1) GitHub App installs with one-click from Marketplace. 2) Bot comments on PR with spec score within 60 seconds of PR creation. 3) Comment includes per-dimension breakdown and actionable suggestions. 4) Configurable threshold via .speclint.yml in repo root. Tags: integration, github, distribution. Constraints: Must handle GitHub API rate limits (5000 req/hr for apps). PR descriptions over 10K chars truncated with warning. Assumptions: GitHub App (not OAuth App) for better permissions model. Verification: Create a test PR with a mediocre description, confirm bot comments within 60 seconds with correct score. Update PR description to address feedback, confirm bot re-scores within 60 seconds and score improves.
